case_title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSTION: $44,997.00 U.S. ET AL

docket_entries.description: THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: RESPONDENT PROPERTY $44,997.00 US CURRENCY IS FORFIETED TO THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA PURSUANT TO HEALTH AND SAFETY CODE SEC. 11488.5(B)(1) AND SHALL BE DISTRIBUTED AS SET FORTH THE THE HEALTH AND SAFETY CODE SEC. 11489. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR DEFAULT JUDGMENT OF FORFEITURE; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES; DECLARATIONS OF VICTORIA ROBINSON, JEFFREY OSPITAL, AND MAITE KVARNA; EXHIBITS (TRANSACTION ID # 77025638) FILED BY PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA HEARING SET FOR OCT-17-2025 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 301
DECLARATION OF VICTORIA ROBINSON IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR DEFAULT JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 77025638) FILED BY PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE OCT-17-2025 HEARING RE: PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR DEFAULT JUDGMENT OF FORFEITURE; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR DEFAULT JUDGMENT OF FORFEITURE IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PREVIOUSLY SUBMITTED PROPOSED DEFAULT JUDGMENT, BUT ALSO NEEDS A PROPOSED ORDER GRANTING THE MOTION. ORDER SUBMITTED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/HEK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment Of Forfeiture; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Victoria Robinson, Jeffrey Ospital, And Maite Kvarna; Exhibits
Off Calendar Oct-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Default Judgment Of Forfeiture.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner People of the State of California's motion for default judgment of forfeiture is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. The court will sign the previously submitted proposed default judgment, but also needs a proposed order granting the motion.

Order submitted.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)
